ref: But if you reject me, I will tell my Father in heaven you don't belong to me. Not Peace, but Trouble (Luke
text: Mɔna walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ te lɛɛ maŋ do denibalaŋ siaman nyi me yɛɛ ɔ Gbeŋgyoo e ya nɛ, maŋ mɔ maa bɛɛ e me kya ŋon ɔ kyaa adido nɛ siaman.
translated: The person who is not a diviner but claims to be one, if he is caught, he will be fined by the chief and his family will also be fined.

ref: Jesus told them to go, and they went out of the men and into the pigs. All at once the pigs rushed down the steep bank into the lake and drowned.
text: Keŋte Yesu yako feliŋkumɛɛ ŋan nyiaa, “Ɛ lee ta.” Mena dɔɔ feliŋkumɛɛ ŋan leewɔ balaŋ baŋ man gyu ka gyoo kekuiriise baŋ man, te be dikpii keŋ pou yeuwɔ tisi bula kyoloŋkyoloŋ keŋ gyu ka gyoo loŋ keŋ man te be munuwɔ pou.
translated: When Jesus saw the crowds, he went up the mountain. And when he sat down, his disciples came to him. And he opened his mouth and taught them, saying...

ref: While Jesus was still speaking to the crowds, his mother and brothers came and stood outside because they wanted to talk with him.
text: Yesu ke gyakaa ɔ ne kolosi fa balaŋ baŋ nɛ, ɔ naa na ɔ naabia kɔŋawɔ kaa seŋ debɔɔ man keŋ bɛɛ gyae gbɛɛ baa kolosi na e.
translated: Jesus did not go to Kolosi that day, he went to Kɔŋawɔ to see if the people of Debɔɔ had also come to Kolosi.

ref: At once, Jesus said to them, “Don't worry! I am Jesus. Don't be afraid.”
text: Debokenaŋ man te Yesu kolosiwɔ fa wɔ nyi, “Ɛ nyiŋ konɔɔ, ɛ na yee ya, maŋ e.”
translated: Debokenaŋ said to Jesus, "It is not good, it is not right, it is not so."

ref: I came to turn sons against their fathers, daughters against their mothers, and daughters-in-law against their mothers-in-law.
text: Bia baala baa koro seŋ tia be kyaɔ, na bia alebia mɔ ka koro seŋ tia be naɔ, na bia ala mɔ ka koro seŋ tia ba awuna.
translated: If you don't know how to do it, and you don't know how to do it, and you don't know how to do it at all.

ref: When Herod found out that the wise men from the east had tricked him, he was very angry. He gave orders for his men to kill all the boys who lived in or near Bethlehem and were two years old and younger. This was based on what he had learned from the wise men.
text: Gyoo Herode kena nyi Siakaralatena baŋ ta mo adao di ɔ dɔɔ, keŋ be ta bese kɔŋ kaa mo ɔ gyaŋ bela ya nɛ ɔ gyeŋa baŋ nideli. Keŋte ɔ kpila ɔ yoonɔɔtena nyi baa gyu Bɛtelɛhɛm donɔɔ man, na ke donɔɔsebia ŋan benaa na ke nɛ, na baa koe bia baala baŋ pou be ta bee ta kela kulutooneŋ ala ya nɛ, nawolo nyi mena debaŋ kenaŋ man te Siakaralatena baŋ yako e nyi be naa kyolobii ŋon.
translated: Herod was very angry when he realized that the wise men had deceived him. He sent men to Bethlehem and ordered them to kill all the boys two years old and younger, according to the time he had learned from the wise men. This fulfilled what the prophet Jeremiah had spoken.

ref: As bad as you are, you still know how to give good gifts to your children. But your heavenly Father is even more ready to give good things to people who ask.
text: Akpaa ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ yɛɛ nombiakumɛɛyɛɛrawɔ koraŋ gyeŋ abɔɔ kpaakpaa ka mo fa ɛ bia na, de yaa sena te ɛ kya ŋon ɔ kyaa adido nɛ be gyae waa mo abɔɔ kpaakpaa ŋan kela kɛŋa nɛ fa baŋ bɛɛ sola e nɛ ya.
translated: The child's mother said that the child's illness is not serious, but if you don't take the child to the hospital, it will get worse, and the child will die, so you should take the child to the hospital now before it's too late.

ref: So God's promise came true, just as the prophet Isaiah had said, “These people will listen and listen, but never understand. They will look and look, but never see.
text: Nombia akɔŋaŋ balaŋ kɛwɔ dɔɔ yela Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Yesaya nombia ŋan ɔ be yako see nɛ kɔŋawɔ ampaŋ. Yesaya yakowɔ nyiaa, “Balaŋ kɛwɔ baa tei tei, mɔna bɔɔ gyae baa nyii nombia ŋenaŋ man ya. Te baa kɛo kɛo, mɔna bɔɔ gyae baa naa kolo ya.
translated: The Lord said to Isaiah, "Go and tell Hezekiah, 'This is what the Lord, the God of your ancestor David, says: I have heard your prayer and seen your tears; I will add fifteen years to your life. And I will deliver you and this city from the hand of the king of Assyria. I will defend this city for my sake and for the sake of my servant David.'" Isaiah went and told Hezekiah, "This is what the Lord says: I will do what you have asked. I will add fifteen years to your life. And I will deliver you and this city from the hand of the king of Assyria. I will defend this city."

ref: The people of all nations will be brought before him, and he will separate them, as shepherds separate their sheep from their goats.
text: Tɛɛle kei balaŋ pou baa kaa yilaa me gyaŋ, na maa kpase be man akpeŋ ala ŋgba mena keŋ wonembia kɛɛlere ne kpase asoose lee namense man nɛ.
translated: The child is very happy because he has found a new friend, but his mother warns him not to trust strangers easily and to always be careful with people he doesn't know well.

ref: Now hurry! Tell his disciples he has been raised to life and is on his way to Galilee. Go there, and you will see him. This is what I came to tell you.”
text: na ɛ ke ta bileŋbileŋ ke yako ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa ‘Ɔ te fom lee woya man te ɔ te kyaŋ ŋon gbɛɛ ɔ ne gyu Galelia tɛɛle man. Botɔɔ te ɛ kena e. Kei yɛna nombii keŋ me dana maa yako ŋon.’ ”
translated: And he went about all Galilee, teaching in their synagogues and preaching the gospel of the kingdom, and healing all manner of sickness and all manner of disease among the people. And his fame went throughout all Syria: and they brought unto him all sick people that were taken with divers diseases and torments.

ref: If your hand or foot causes you to sin, chop it off and throw it away! You would be better off to go into life paralyzed or lame than to have two hands or two feet and be thrown into the fire that never goes out.
text: Nyi akpaa n nyiŋmaa yaa n nae ne yele la te n ne yɛɛ dukum na kara ke leki. Nawolo nyi, dei nyi nya lee kadeŋ na n ka gyoo Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man, kela nyi baa mo neŋ nyiŋmaase ala yaa nawɔɔ ala ka do boalaŋ keŋ ke bɛɛ yem ya nɛ man.
translated: I have not seen my friend for a long time, and I miss him. Now, if I go to the village and don't find Wurubuarɛ there, then I will know that my friend has truly gone to the farm and will not return until evening.

ref: “I feel sorry for these people. They have been with me for three days, and they don't have anything to eat.
text: “Balaŋ kɛwɔ nombia dana maŋ waraŋase. Nawolo nyi gyɛŋ weeya kpaakpaa atooro yaa ŋgba be kyaa me gyaŋ, te kolo be doo baa di ya nɛ.
translated: The child was not happy with the food his mother gave him. He said that the food was too hard and he could not eat it, so he left it and went to play.

ref: Later the disciples went to Jesus in private and asked him, “Why couldn't we force out the demon?”
text: Kenaŋ wɔle te Yesu kaseela baŋ gyuuwɔ ɔ gyaŋ, te be baake e lee yenaŋ bɔɔse e nyiaa, “Weera dɔɔ te daa te tale gegi feliŋkum keŋ lee bu ŋon man ya?”
translated: When Jesus saw him lying there and knew that he had already been there a long time, he said to him, “Do you want to be healed?”

ref: Jesus told them, “Only those people who have been given the gift of staying single can accept this teaching.
text: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Nombia ŋan mɛɛ yako ŋon nɛ, na walaŋ kamasɛ ne gyae la waa tale lɛɛ ŋa do ya, gyisɛ balaŋ baŋ Wurubuarɛ ba mo mena bɛrɛɛ kenaŋ boele wɔ nɛ baageŋ.
translated: When Jesus saw this, he said, “You have not believed because you have seen me; blessed are those who have not seen and yet have believed.”

ref: When he saw the crowds, he felt sorry for them. They were confused and helpless, like sheep without a shepherd.
text: Ɔ kena balaŋ dikpii keŋ nɛ, be nombia yɛɛ e waraŋase. Nawolo nyi bɛɛ yeyɛɛ te bɔɔ dana walaŋ ŋon waa lɛɛ wɔ ya. Be nombia yɛɛ ŋgba namense baŋ bɔɔ dana gbeŋgyoo ya nɛ.
translated: When the child was born, they named him Waraŋase. Nawolo said that he would grow up to be a great hunter or a great farmer. They named him Waraŋase because of the great things he would do in the future.

ref: When Herod found out that the wise men from the east had tricked him, he was very angry. He gave orders for his men to kill all the boys who lived in or near Bethlehem and were two years old and younger. This was based on what he had learned from the wise men.

Keŋte ɔ kpila ɔ yoonɔɔtena nyi baa gyu Bɛtelɛhɛm donɔɔ man, na ke donɔɔsebia ŋan benaa na ke nɛ, na baa koe bia baala baŋ pou be ta bee ta kela kulutooneŋ ala ya nɛ, nawolo nyi mena debaŋ kenaŋ man te Siakaralatena baŋ yako e nyi be naa kyolobii ŋon.
translated: Herod was very troubled when he heard this, and everyone in Jerusalem was troubled with him. So he called together all the leading priests and teachers of religious law and asked them, "Where is the Messiah supposed to be born?" "In Bethlehem in Judea," they said, "for this is what the prophet wrote: 'And you, O Bethlehem in the land of Judah, are not least among the ruling cities of Judah, for a ruler will come from you who will be the shepherd for my people Israel.'"

ref: As bad as you are, you still know how to give good gifts to your children. But your heavenly Father is even more ready to give good things to people who ask.
text: Akpaa ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ yɛɛ nombiakumɛɛyɛɛrawɔ koraŋ gyeŋ abɔɔ kpaakpaa ka mo fa ɛ bia na, de yaa sena te ɛ kya ŋon ɔ kyaa adido nɛ be gyae waa mo abɔɔ kpaakpaa ŋan kela kɛŋa nɛ fa baŋ bɛɛ sola e nɛ ya.
translated: The man who is always ready to help others in times of need, even if it means sacrificing his own comfort, is truly a rare gem, and we should all strive to be like him.

ref: So God's promise came true, just as the prophet Isaiah had said, “These people will listen and listen, but never understand. They will look and look, but never see.

translated: The Lord's message came to Isaiah son of Amoz during the time when Uzziah, Jotham, Ahaz, and Hezekiah were kings of Judah. Isaiah saw a vision, “I saw the Lord sitting on a high and lofty throne, and the hem of his robe filled the temple. Above him stood seraphs, each with six wings: with two they covered their faces, with two they covered their feet, and with two they flew.

ref: The people of all nations will be brought before him, and he will separate them, as shepherds separate their sheep from their goats.
text: Tɛɛle kei balaŋ pou baa kaa yilaa me gyaŋ, na maa kpase be man akpeŋ ala ŋgba mena keŋ wonembia kɛɛlere ne kpase asoose lee namense man nɛ.
translated: The child who is not obedient to his parents, when he grows up and gets married, he will not be able to manage his home well, and his wife will not respect him.

ref: Now hurry! Tell his disciples he has been raised to life and is on his way to Galilee. Go there, and you will see him. This is what I came to tell you.”
text: na ɛ ke ta bileŋbileŋ ke yako ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa ‘Ɔ te fom lee woya man te ɔ te kyaŋ ŋon gbɛɛ ɔ ne gyu Galelia tɛɛle man. Botɔɔ te ɛ kena e. Kei yɛna nombii keŋ me dana maa yako ŋon.’ ”
translated: And he said to them, "You will surely say this proverb to me, 'Physician, heal yourself! Whatever we have heard done in Capernaum, do also here in your hometown.' " And he said, "Truly, I say to you, no prophet is acceptable in his hometown."
